audit_status.source_base: Greek Gospel text tradition, draft aligned to public-domain comparison layer later
audit_status.review_note: Compact complete draft reviewed for empty tomb, women witnesses, Peter, Emmaus, opened Scriptures, recognized in broken bread, resurrection appearance, opened minds, mission, promise, Bethany blessing/ascension.

verses.restored: On the first day of the week, at deep dawn, they came to the tomb bringing the spices they had prepared.
They found the stone rolled away from the tomb.
Entering, they did not find the body of the Lord Jesus.
It happened, while they were perplexed about this, look, two men stood by them in dazzling clothing.
As they became afraid and bowed their faces to the earth, they said to them, Why do you seek the living one among the dead?
He is not here, but was raised. Remember how he spoke to you while still in Galilee,
saying that the Son of Humanity must be handed over into hands of sinful/misaligned humans, be crucified, and on the third day rise.
They remembered his words.
Returning from the tomb, they reported all these things to the eleven and to all the rest.
Now they were Mary Magdalene, Joanna, Mary the mother of James, and the other women with them; they were saying these things to the apostles.
These words appeared before them like nonsense, and they were not trusting them.
But Peter, rising, ran to the tomb; stooping, he sees the linen cloths alone, and he went away to himself, marveling at what had happened.
Look, two of them were traveling that same day to a village named Emmaus, about sixty stadia from Jerusalem.
They were speaking with one another about all these things that had happened.
It happened, while they were speaking and discussing, Jesus himself drew near and traveled with them.
But their eyes were held back so that they did not recognize him.
He said to them, What are these words you are exchanging with one another as you walk? They stood still, looking sad.
One named Cleopas answered him, Are you the only one dwelling near Jerusalem who does not know the things that happened in it in these days?
He said to them, What things? They said to him, The things concerning Jesus the Nazarene, who became a man prophet powerful in work and word before the Divine and all the people,
and how our chief priests and rulers handed him over to a judgment of death and crucified him.
But we had hoped that he was the one about to redeem Israel. Yet with all these things, this is the third day since these things happened.
But also certain women from among us astonished us, having come early to the tomb.
Not finding his body, they came saying they had also seen a vision of messengers who say he lives.
Some of those with us went to the tomb and found it just as the women said, but him they did not see.
He said to them, O uncomprehending ones, and slow of heart to trust upon all that the prophets spoke.
Was it not necessary for the Messiah to suffer these things and enter into his glory?
Beginning from Moses and from all the prophets, he interpreted to them in all the Scriptures the things concerning himself.
They drew near to the village where they were going, and he acted as though he were going farther.
They urged him strongly, saying, Remain with us, because it is toward evening and the day has already declined. He went in to remain with them.
It happened, when he reclined with them, taking the bread, he blessed, broke, and gave it to them.
Their eyes were opened, and they recognized him; and he became invisible from them.
They said to one another, Was not our heart burning within us as he was speaking to us on the way, as he was opening the Scriptures to us?
Rising that same hour, they returned to Jerusalem and found the eleven gathered together and those with them,
saying, The Lord was truly raised and appeared to Simon.
They described the things on the way and how he was known to them in the breaking of the bread.
As they were speaking these things, he himself stood in their midst and said to them, Peace to you.
Startled and becoming afraid, they thought they were beholding a spirit.
He said to them, Why are you troubled, and why do reasonings rise in your heart?
See my hands and my feet, that I myself am; touch me and see, because a spirit does not have flesh and bones as you behold me having.
[Saying this, he showed them the hands and the feet.]
While they were still not trusting from joy and were marveling, he said to them, Do you have anything edible here?
They gave him a piece of broiled fish.
Taking it, he ate before them.
He said to them, These are my words that I spoke to you while still with you: that it is necessary for all things written in the Law of Moses, the Prophets, and Psalms concerning me to be fulfilled.
Then he opened their mind to understand the Scriptures.
He said to them, Thus it has been written: the Messiah suffers and rises from the dead on the third day,
and turning/realignment for release of sins/misalignments is proclaimed in his name to all nations, beginning from Jerusalem.
You are witnesses of these things.
Look, I am sending the promise of my Father upon you; but you remain in the city until you are clothed with power from on high.
He led them out as far as Bethany, and lifting up his hands, he blessed them.
It happened, as he blessed them, he parted from them and was carried up into heaven.
They worshiped him and returned to Jerusalem with great joy.
They were continually in the temple, blessing the Divine.
